red_under_diagnosis.failure_signature: test_f4_happy_path_project_create_and_list: flow cannot compose — CT9 (C6->C3) declares kind:async_event requiring Web Ingress to enqueue the project page request and not block, but no broker is wired and an asynchronous dispatch cannot return server-rendered HTML inside the synchronous request/response cycle (ADR-0004 MPA/SSR, INV6).

flaky_check.evidence: Failure asserts a structural composition impossibility: CT9.kind=async_event requires a broker that cannot exist under INV6 (single-server synchronous, no background job queues — A13/ADR-0001) and cannot return rendered HTML in the synchronous MPA request cycle (ADR-0004). Three identical signatures, zero variance across attempts. Deterministic.

reflection_pass.finding: CT9 declares kind:async_event and shape 'Web Ingress publishes the project page request onto a message queue and does not block; rendered HTML delivered out of band.' This contradicts INV6 + A13 (single-server synchronous request cycle, no background job queues) and ADR-0004 (MPA/SSR — the server renders and returns the HTML inside the request/response). The frozen slice oracle resolves CT9 as a synchronous dispatch (test_F4.py calls dispatch_project_request and asserts a rendered response in-cycle; conftest mocks no broker). CT9 still lists honors_inv:[INV6] — inconsistent with kind:async_event. Wiring a broker would violate INV6 and break the frozen slice oracle. CT9.kind=async_event is the defect — unbuildable as frozen. The implementation already dispatches synchronously; this is the wrong contract, not a wiring gap (the provisional my-code-wiring hint is a mis-classification).

skeleton_fidelity.finding: The defect is in the SLICE contract CT9 (introduced this slice, role:introduced-seam). The frozen skeleton — CT1/CT8, the F1 flow test, and the skeleton composition root (wsgi.py) — needs no change to resolve it; the slice flow F4 inherits the skeleton baseline by reference and does not reshape it. Slice-local Phase-3 re-derive; no skeleton ripple (H14). Not a skeleton-fidelity breach.

routable_diagnosis.change_request: Change slice contract CT9.kind from 'async_event' to 'sync_api'. Restore CT9.shape to a synchronous in-process dispatch: Web Ingress routes the authenticated HTTP request in-process to Project Management, which returns a server-rendered HTML response within the request/response cycle (project list/detail/create/edit/delete). Honors INV6 (synchronous, no broker) + ADR-0004 (MPA/SSR). Rationale: INV6 + A13 prohibit background job queues; ADR-0004 mandates server-rendered HTML returned in-cycle; the frozen slice oracle already materializes CT9 as a synchronous dispatch; the implementation already dispatches synchronously. CT9 is a slice-introduced seam — re-derive at the S4 HLD increment; the frozen skeleton is untouched (no ripple). No other CT9 field requires change.
